channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Aerospike Vector Search adds self-healing live indexes
item.description: Real-time database provider Aerospike has updated its Aerospike Vector Search database extension for powering generative AI applications. The update offers new indexing and storage capabilities for real-time accuracy, scalability, and ease of use for developers, Aerospike said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Corps of Engineers will look to save 150-year-old lighthouse from crumbling into the Hudson River
item.description: Efforts to save an imperiled 150-year-old lighthouse in New York are getting a boost with federal officials announcing new funding to study ways to protect the structure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Founder mode can’t work. Be maximalist
item.description: The tech industry was recently abuzz with a post from Y Combinator founder Paul Graham on “founder mode.
item.dc:creator: Bipul Sinha, Fast Company

item.title: The case for private clouds for data management in government
item.description: When it comes to taking advantage of AI, storing data in a cost-effective, massively scalable private or hybrid cloud solution is often the best choice. The post The case for private clouds for data management in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: This Mark Cuban-backed startup is helping creators monetize their audiences
item.description: Falon Fatemi first met Mark Cuban in 2011 at South By Southwest, where the two bonded over ideas about untapped opportunities in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Trump says he has a 'warm spot' in his heart for TikTok as app faces ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he has a “warm spot” in his heart for TikTok as the popular social media app faces down a potential ban next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: A U.S. Senate committee says at least two internal Amazon studies have found a relationship between worker speed and injuries in the online retailer's warehouses.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s cybersecurity strategy: Building a resilient digital future
item.description: As Saudi Arabia accelerates its digital transformation, cybersecurity has become a cornerstone of its national strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Weaponizing generative AI
item.description: Well, that didn’t last long. Generative AI has existed just a few short years, but already we seem to be alternating between bouts of disillusionment and euphoria.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: La Consejería de Salud de Cantabria, galardonada en los ‘CIO 100 Awards’ como Administración Pública del Año
item.description: La Consejería de Salud de Cantabria ha sido reconocida como Administración Pública del Año en el marco de la celebración de los CIO 100 Awards Spain 2024.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SAP systems increasingly targeted by cyber attackers
item.description: A review of four years of threat intelligence data, presented Friday at Black Hat by Yvan Genuer, a senior security researcher at Onapsis, reports a spike in hacker interest in breaking into enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems from SAP in 2020 that was sustained until the end of 2023.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Schools are cutting bus service for children. Parents are turning to ride-hailing apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcmurray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: How you can prepare your TikTok account for the upcoming ban
item.description: If you’re one of the 170 million TikTok users in the U.S., it’s time to start making sure your account is properly secured and updated ahead of the impending January ban.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court has left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SAP: Latest news and insights
item.description: SAP is an enterprise software vendor based in Walldorf, Germany. Its cloud and on-premises enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, including S/4HANA, helps organizations manage their business operations and customer relations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta wants Threads to be more like Bluesky. Again
item.description: The surest sign a social media platform is winning: when Meta starts cribbing notes from it. Stories only arrived on Instagram in 2016 after Snapchat proved the ephemeral concept had some heat.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Sam Altman to donate $1 million in personal funds to Trump's inauguration fund
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-elect Trump's inaugural fund, joining various other tech leaders who have made similar contributions this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online ‘constantly,’ Pew report finds
item.description: YouTube was the single most popular platform teenagers used — 90% said they watched videos on the site, down slightly from 95% in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: As AI makes its way into movies, Duncan Crabtree-Ireland is fighting for actors’ rights
item.description: After years of advocating for actors’ collective rights, Duncan Crabtree-Ireland is finally getting his own close-up.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Snow, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft .NET Community Toolkit backs partial properties
item.description: Microsoft has released .NET Community Toolkit 8.4, with support for partial properties in MVVM Toolkit source generators as well as new MVVM Toolkit diagnostic analyzers. The update was unveiled December 12. Source code for the .NET Community Toolkit can be found on GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Global internet traffic surges 17% as post-quantum cryptography adoption grows
item.description: Overall internet traffic continues to grow year after year, with no slowdown in sight. Cloudflare released its annual Cloudflare Radar Year in Review report this week, providing insights into the state of the internet in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sweden gets one of the EU’s seven AI factories
item.description: The new Swedish AI factory is named Mimer and is one of seven now selected by the EU. Mimer is one of five of these AI factories that will now have a brand new AI-optimized supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From surveillance to safety: How Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV is reshaping security
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell
item.description: [Task Force Lima] had made so much progress and had such concrete recommendations that we moved to stand up this cell for actual execution,” said Radha Plumb. The post DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response
item.description: When it comes to cybersecurity, the more data you have, the easier it is to identify anomalies and stop adversaries, shares Agriculture CISO Ignatius Liberto. The post Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4
